title: Parsanol 0.3.0: Captures, Scopes, and Dynamic Atoms
summary: Major release adding capture extraction, isolated scopes, and context-sensitive parsing.

content.text: Parsanol 0.3.0 introduces three powerful new features for extracting and managing parsed data: Capture Atoms, Scope Atoms, and Dynamic Atoms.
Capture Atoms
Extract named values from your input, like named groups in regular expressions but integrated into the parsing grammar.
Scope Atoms
Create isolated capture contexts for nested parsing. Captures made inside a scope are discarded when the scope exits, preventing capture pollution in recursive structures.
Dynamic Atoms
Runtime-determined parsing via callbacks. Enable context-sensitive parsing where the grammar itself depends on input or previously captured values.
Backend Compatibility
What's Changed

items: New ParseResult methods: capture_names(), get_capture(), captures()
Streaming parser capture persistence across chunks

title: Parsanol 0.2.0: Streaming Parser and Benchmarks
summary: Added streaming parser for large files, comprehensive benchmarks, and improved documentation.

content.text: Parsanol 0.2.0 brings streaming parser support, comprehensive benchmarking tools, and significant documentation improvements.
Streaming Parser
Parse large files without loading them into memory. The streaming parser processes input in configurable chunks with a sliding window for backtracking support.
Benchmark Suite
New comprehensive benchmark suite comparing all backends across different grammar types. The benchmarks demonstrate 18-44x performance improvement over pure Ruby parsers.
What's Changed

items: StreamingParser with ChunkConfig for large file support
Improved error messages with source context

title: Parsanol 0.1.0: Initial Release
summary: First public release of Parsanol, a modern parser combinator library for Rust and Ruby.

content.text: Parsanol is a modern parser combinator library that combines the elegance of PEG parsing with the performance of Rust. It provides multiple parsing backends, rich error reporting, and seamless Ruby bindings.
Core Features

items: Packrat parser with O(n) complexity guarantee
Arena allocation for zero-copy AST construction
Derive macros for automatic AST conversion
Ruby FFI bindings with 5 integration approaches

content.text: Multiple Backends
Ruby Integration
Five integration approaches from pure Ruby to ZeroCopy+Slice for maximum flexibility and performance. Parslet-compatible API makes migration straightforward.
Example Grammars
30+ complete examples included for real-world formats: JSON, CSV, XML, TOML, YAML, Markdown, URL, Email, S-expressions, and more.
